title: Arithmetic Progression
text: What is the next term in the series: 3, 7, 11, 15, 19, ?
explanation:
The series is an arithmetic progression with a common difference of 4. Each term is obtained by adding 4 to the previous term: 3+4=7, 7+4=11, 11+4=15, 15+4=19. The next term is 19+4=23.

Common mistake: Adding 3 or 2 instead of 4 gives 22 or 21. Adding 8 (double the difference) gives 27.

title: Halving Pattern
text: What is the next term in the series: 128, 64, 32, 16, ?
explanation:
Each term is obtained by dividing the previous term by 2: 128÷2=64, 64÷2=32, 32÷2=16. The next term is 16÷2=8.

Common mistake: Continuing the pattern too aggressively (dividing again by 2 to get 4) or misreading the direction (thinking the difference is -112, -32, -16, and applying +8 to get 24).

title: Perfect Squares
text: What is the next term in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
explanation:
These are perfect squares of consecutive natural numbers: 1²=1, 2²=4, 3²=9, 4²=16, 5²=25. The next term is 6²=36.

Common mistake: Choosing 49 (7²) skips a position. Choosing 35 or 30 results from arithmetic difference extension (+3, +5, +7, +9, so +11 would give 36, but miscounting gives 35 or 30).

title: Letter Positions
text: What is the next term in the series: A, C, E, G, I, ?
explanation:
Each letter advances by 2 positions in the alphabet: A(1), C(3), E(5), G(7), I(9). The next letter is K(11) — I(9)+2=11.

Common mistake: Advancing by only 1 gives J. Advancing by 3 gives L. Reversing direction gives H.

title: Powers of Two
text: What is the next term in the series: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ?
explanation:
Each term doubles the previous term: 1×2=2, 2×2=4, 4×2=8, 8×2=16. The next term is 16×2=32. These are powers of 2: 2⁰, 2¹, 2², 2³, 2⁴.

Common mistake: Choosing 64 (2⁶) skips 2⁵. Choosing 24 or 30 comes from extending the difference pattern (+1, +2, +4, +8) by adding 8 again or 14 instead of doubling.

title: Fibonacci Sequence
text: What is the next term in the series: 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, ?
explanation:
Each term (from the third onwards) is the sum of the two preceding terms: 2+3=5, 3+5=8, 5+8=13. The next term is 8+13=21.

Common mistake: Adding the wrong pair (13+5=18) treats the wrong previous terms. Reverting to the first difference (13+2=15) loses the pattern. Doubling (13×2=26) misreads the relationship as multiplicative.

title: Triangular Numbers
text: What is the next term in the series: 1, 3, 6, 10, 15, ?
explanation:
These are triangular numbers: T₁=1, T₂=3, T₃=6, T₄=10, T₅=15. Each term adds one more than the previous increment: +2, +3, +4, +5. The next increment is +6: 15+6=21. The formula is T_n = n(n+1)/2.

Common mistake: Adding 3 again (15+3=18) assumes the same difference repeats. Adding 5 (15+5=20) uses the previous increment without increasing it. Choosing 25 confuses this with perfect squares.

title: Accelerating Differences
text: What is the next term in the series: 3, 5, 9, 15, 23, ?
explanation:
The differences between consecutive terms increase by 2 each time: 5-3=2, 9-5=4, 15-9=6, 23-15=8. The next difference is +10: 23+10=33.

Common mistake: Repeating the last difference (23+8=31) instead of increasing it. Increasing by too much (23+12=35) results from adding 4 instead of 2 to the difference. Choosing 28 (23+5) reverts to the first difference.

title: Geometric Progression
text: What is the next term in the series: 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
explanation:
Each term is multiplied by 3: 2×3=6, 6×3=18, 18×3=54. The next term is 54×3=162.

Common mistake: Halving the multiplier to 2 (54×2=108) assumes the ratio decreases. Adding the previous term (54+18=72) reverts to an additive relationship. Doubling the multiplier to 4 (54×4=216) overestimates the ratio.

title: Squares Minus One
text: What is the next term in the series: 0, 3, 8, 15, 24, ?
explanation:
The pattern is n²-1: 1²-1=0, 2²-1=3, 3²-1=8, 4²-1=15, 5²-1=24. The next term is 6²-1=35.

Common mistake: Choosing 36 (6²) gets the square right but forgets to subtract 1. Choosing 33 (6²-3) subtracts the wrong amount. Choosing 30 extends an arbitrary difference pattern without identifying the structure.

title: Increasing Squares
text: What is the next term in the series: 5, 10, 17, 26, 37, ?
explanation:
The pattern is n²+1 shifted: 2²+1=5, 3²+1=10, 4²+1=17, 5²+1=26, 6²+1=37. The next term is 7²+1=50.

Common mistake: Computing the square correctly but forgetting the +1 (7²=49) and getting 47 or 48 from difference extension. Choosing 55 incorrectly applies 7²+6 instead of 7²+1.

title: Decreasing Increments
text: What is the next term in the series: 20, 15, 11, 8, 6, ?
explanation:
The differences between consecutive terms decrease by 1 each time: 20-15=5, 15-11=4, 11-8=3, 8-6=2. The next difference is -1: 6-1=5.

Common mistake: Repeating the last difference (6-2=4) instead of decreasing it. Applying too large a decrement (6-3=3) double-decreases. Reversing to positive (6+1=7) misreads the direction.

title: Squares Plus One
text: What is the next term in the series: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?
explanation:
The pattern is n²+1: 1²+1=2, 2²+1=5, 3²+1=10, 4²+1=17, 5²+1=26. The next term is 6²+1=37.

Common mistake: Computing the square without the +1 (6²=36) is the classic off-by-one trap. Choosing 35 assumes n²-1 instead of n²+1. Choosing 41 (6²+5) uses an incorrect increment.

title: Perfect Cubes
text: What is the next term in the series: 1, 8, 27, 64, 125, ?
explanation:
These are perfect cubes: 1³=1, 2³=8, 3³=27, 4³=64, 5³=125. The next term is 6³=216.

Common mistake: Choosing 256 (4⁴ or 2⁸) confuses cubes with powers of 4 or 2. Choosing 215 (6³-1) is an off-by-one error. Choosing 180 results from extending arithmetic differences without identifying the cubic pattern.

title: Alternating Signs
text: What is the next term in the series: -1, 2, -3, 4, -5, ?
explanation:
The absolute values follow the natural numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Odd positions have a negative sign. The next term is position 6 (even), so it is positive: +6.

Common mistake: Continuing the negative sign (-6) assumes all terms are negative. Using 5 confuses the value with the previous absolute value. Choosing -7 skips a position or misreads the sign pattern.

title: Factorial Sequence
text: What is the next term in the series: 1, 2, 6, 24, 120, ?
explanation:
These are factorials: 1! = 1, 2! = 2, 3! = 6, 4! = 24, 5! = 120. The next term is 6! = 720.

Common mistake: Choosing 600 (120×5) knows the multiplier increases but miscalculates 120×6 as 600. Choosing 240 (120×2) halves the multiplier. Choosing 840 (120×7) overestimates by using 7 as the multiplier instead of 6.

title: Compound Pattern
text: What is the next term in the series: 4, 10, 28, 82, ?
explanation:
Each term follows the pattern: multiply by 3 and subtract 2: 4×3-2=10, 10×3-2=28, 28×3-2=82. The next term is 82×3-2=244.

Common mistake: Doubling instead of tripling (82×2=164) catches those who see a pattern but pick the wrong multiplier. Forgetting to subtract 2 (82×3=246) gives the raw triple. Choosing 84 (82+2) reverses the operation to addition.

title: Triple Operation
text: What is the next term in the series: 3, 8, 22, 63, ?
explanation:
The pattern is: multiply by 3 and subtract a decreasing amount: 3×3-1=8, 8×3-2=22, 22×3-3=63. The subtracted amount increases by 1 each time. The next term is 63×3-4=185.

Common mistake: Forgetting the decreasing subtractor (63×3=189) gives the raw triple. Halving the multiplier (63×2=126) misreads the scale. Getting 172 (63×3-17) uses the wrong subtracted amount.

title: Double and Add
text: What is the next term in the series: 7, 16, 34, 70, ?
explanation:
Each term follows: multiply by 2 and add 2: 7×2+2=16, 16×2+2=34, 34×2+2=70. The next term is 70×2+2=142.

Common mistake: Forgetting the +2 (70×2=140) gives a near-miss answer. Adding the previous term (70+34=104) assumes an additive Fibonacci-like pattern. Tripling instead of doubling (70×3=210) overestimates the multiplier.

title: Squares and Cubes
text: What is the next term in the series: 2, 12, 36, 80, ?
explanation:
Each term is the sum of a square and a cube: 1²+1³=1+1=2, 2²+2³=4+8=12, 3²+3³=9+27=36, 4²+4³=16+64=80. The next term is 5²+5³=25+125=150.

Common mistake: Choosing 125 (5³) sees only the cube pattern and ignores the square component. Choosing 130 miscalculates 25+125=150 as 130. Choosing 180 (5²×5+5³×5³) applies an incorrect multiplicative operation.
